q: Neonicotinoids affect insect nervous systems by which mechanism?
options: They block voltage-gated Na+ channels, preventing action potentials in insect neurons
They bind irreversibly to nicotinic acetylcholine receptors on insect neurons, causing continuous excitation
They inhibit acetylcholinesterase, preventing ACh breakdown at neuromuscular junctions
They mimic GABA and cause hyperpolarization of insect motor neurons
explanation: Neonicotinoids are nicotinic acetylcholine receptor agonists; they bind and continuously activate these receptors in insect nervous systems, causing paralysis and death.
